reason: Both answers reach the same correct top-line recommendation (don't match) and cover the core reasoning: can't win a price war against deeper pockets, segment/differentiate rather than cut broadly, VC-subsidized pricing is often unsustainable, and selective/targeted discounting beats blanket cuts. Both end with the same caveat about undifferentiated products. Answer A adds a distinctive point (locking in multi-year contracts to protect NRR and runway) that B lacks. Answer B adds a distinctive point (explicitly connecting the 18-month runway constraint to a suggestion to extend runway via raise/burn cuts) and an extra 'add differentiation fast' action. Each has one added insight not in the other, and both hit the key points with comparable clarity and concision. Neither contains an error that would mislead the asker.
